content.text:
**Project context**

- **Project:** Bonfire House; unaddressed site in **Chatham County, North Carolina**; code summary cites **2018 North Carolina Residential Code** (IRC-based) under Chatham County jurisdiction.  
- **Building:** New **single-family residence** with garage, deck, pool, well, and septic; code sheet classifies it as **R-3 / Type V-B**.  
- **Climate zone:** **4A** (stated).  
- **Drawing scope:** Architectural set includes general/code sheets, site plan, floor plans, roof plan, exterior elevations, building/wall sections, wall and floor/roof assemblies, exterior/deck/interior details, reflected ceiling plans, interior elevations, and schedules; **structural, civil, MEP, landscape, and pool engineering drawings are not provided**.  
- **Specifications provided:** A narrative **materials/applications/allowances booklet**, not a formal CSI division-based project manual.

### A. Internal consistency across sheets

[A-1] | A2.01A, A8.01 | Door tag **11-1** is shown on the Level 1 plan at Bathroom 11 but has no corresponding entry in the door schedule. | Certain  
[A-2] | A8.01, A2.01A-B, A2.02 | Door **10-1** is listed in the door schedule but no door with tag 10-1 appears on the floor plans. | Certain  
[A-3] | A8.01 Room Finish Schedule, A2.01B, A4.01 | Powder Room **14** is scheduled with floor finish **F-4** even though the plan tags it **F-2** and **F-4** is the carpet assembly. | Certain  
[A-4] | A7.02 title block, A2.01B, A8.01 | Interior Elevation 5 on A7.02 is labeled **"Guest Bathroom 19"** even though Room **19** is the Guest Closet and the guest bathroom is Room **18**. | Certain  
[A-5] | A8.01 Room Finish Schedule, A7.10/1-2 | Reading Nook **10** walls are scheduled as **WD-1**, but the section/detail shows **WD-4** tongue-and-groove wall finish with **WD-1** used only for shelves/trim. | High  
[A-6] | A6.01 Lighting Fixture Schedule, A2.01A-B, A3.22, A7.01 | **L7** strip lighting is scheduled with a count of **2** even though it is shown at the kitchen, stair handrail, and reading nook in multiple drawings. | High  
[A-7] | A5.01, A5.10 | Deck drawings are inconsistent on railing heights, with the plan calling out **36"** and **48"** cable railings while the elevations also introduce a **26"** cable railing condition. | High  

### B. Dimensional and geometric integrity

[B-1] | A3.02/1, A2.00, A3.01, A3.10 | The north elevation sets **"PLAN, CRAWL SPACE"** at **501'-0"** while the crawlspace datum is **502'-0"** on the plans, sections, and other elevations. | Certain  
[B-2] | A3.22/1-2, A3.10, A2.01B, A2.02 | The stair riser information shown as **10 equal risers** and **8 equal risers** at **6 11/16"** does not cleanly reconcile to the **10'-0"** Level 1 to Level 2 floor-to-floor height. | Moderate  
[B-3] | A2.01B, A3.21 | The garage slab is shown with a **-1'-0"** spot elevation on the Level 1 plan, but the garage section does not carry a separate garage floor datum to confirm that vertical relationship. | High  
[B-4] | A2.03, A3.02/1, A3.03/2 | The connector/entry roof slope is labeled **1/4" per foot** on the roof plan but **1/8" per foot** on exterior elevations, so the actual pitch is unclear. | Moderate  
[B-5] | A2.00, A3.10, A5.20 | The lowered crawlspace/pool mechanical slab is called out at **-7'-0"** on the crawlspace plan, but the building and pool sections do not show that depressed slab elevation. | High  

### C. Specification-to-drawing alignment

[C-1] | Specs p.3, A8.01 Window Schedule | The specs call for **Andersen 400 Series** windows, but the window schedule is based on **Marvin Ultrex** fixed and casement units. | Certain  
[C-2] | Specs p.3, A8.01 Door Schedule | The specs include exterior door **03-01** in the **Jeld-Wen wood/glass exterior door** package, but the door schedule lists **03-1** as a **metal** door. | Certain  
[C-3] | Specs p.2, A4.00/E-2 | The specs state all conditioned exterior walls are **2x6** framing, but wall type **E-2** is drawn as a **2x8** insulated exterior wall. | Certain  
[C-4] | Specs p.4, A4.00/E-2-E-4 | The specs describe the wall insulation as **R-21 batts with 1" insulated ZIP board**, but the wall assemblies show separate **1 1/2" rigid insulation** over **1/2" plywood sheathing** rather than insulated ZIP. | Certain  
[C-5] | Specs p.4, G0.11 | Ceiling insulation values conflict, with the specs stating **R-49** and the code summary stating **R-53**. | Certain  
[C-6] | Specs p.2, A4.01/F-5, A8.01 Room Finish Schedule | The specs require an **epoxy-coated garage floor**, but the garage floor assembly and finish schedule show only the concrete slab assembly with no epoxy finish identified. | High  
[C-7] | Specs p.3, A5.01, A5.10 | The specs describe the pool enclosure as a **48" wood/frame cable fence** on three sides, but the drawings show a mix of **48"**, **36"**, and **26"** cable railing conditions. | Certain  
[C-8] | Specs p.9, A6.01 | The low-voltage specs require **Cat6a** ethernet cabling, but the reflected ceiling plan notes are labeled **Cat6e**. | Certain  
[C-9] | Specs p.1, A2.03 | The specs describe the roofs as **3"/12 slopes** on the diagonal, but the roof plan includes roof areas labeled **2"/12** and **1/4"/12**. | High  
[C-10] | Specs p.10, A2.00, A5.01 | The specs place the pool mechanical equipment on the side of the pool **opposite the house toward the west end**, but the drawings locate the pool mechanical area adjacent to the house/deck. | High  
[C-11] | G0.01, Specs booklet | Sheet G0.01 directs the contractor to door data in **"Specifications Div. 8,"** but the provided specification booklet is a narrative allowance document with no Division 8 section or door data. | Certain  
[C-12] | G0.11, Specs p.7, A3.01-A3.03 | Hose bib quantity is inconsistent because the code summary lists **5**, the specs call for **3 Aqua Hydrants**, and the elevations depict a different number of hydrant locations. | High  
[C-13] | Specs p.9, A8.01 Door Schedule | The specs distinguish the **master shower door as frameless** and the **guest shower door as semi-framed**, but the door schedule lists both shower doors generically as clear glass doors with no framing distinction. | High  

### D. Building envelope and building science

[D-1] | A2.03, A4.01/R-1, A4.12/4&6, A4.13/5, Specs p.4 | Roof areas drawn at very low slope are covered by the same standing-seam metal roof assembly/product as the main roofs, but no separate low-slope roofing assembly is provided. | High  
[D-2] | A4.12/6 | The roof-intersection detail concentrates water into an internal gutter/valley condition without showing a secondary waterproofing strategy beneath the metal roof system. | High  
[D-3] | A4.11/5, A4.10/6, A5.30/3&5 | Base details do not establish a consistent clearance between cladding/wood components and adjacent grade, paving, or decking, leaving several moisture-sensitive terminations vulnerable to splash-back. | High  
[D-4] | A4.00/E-1, A4.01/F-1, A4.11/5 | The sealed crawlspace enclosure is not shown as a clearly continuous air/thermal/vapor barrier at the stem-wall-to-floor-to-exterior-wall transition. | Moderate  
[D-5] | A4.10/1-8 | Entry hall glazing details over stone/concrete do not show a subsill pan or explicit drainage path at the bases of the custom window and door assemblies. | Moderate  
[D-6] | A4.13/1-4 | The custom corner window details include jamb sections only and omit head and sill waterproofing details for these high-risk envelope conditions. | High  
[D-7] | A5.30/3&5 | Deck-to-foundation details show flashing at the wall but do not indicate any drainage/ventilation strategy for the deck edge condition adjacent to the insulated foundation/enclosure. | Moderate  

### E. Code and life safety

[E-1] | G0.11 | The code summary mixes IRC and IBC concepts and states that a **Type V-B/R-3** building is allowed **unlimited area per story**, making the stated code basis unreliable. | High  
[E-2] | A2.01B, A4.00, A4.01 | The garage-to-house wall separation is not identified as a code-compliant fire separation assembly and is shown only with generic wall types. | High  
[E-3] | A3.10/2, A4.01/F-1 | The floor/ceiling assembly over the garage is shown as a generic **F-1** assembly with **1/2" gypsum board** and is not identified as a garage separation assembly for habitable space above. | High  
[E-4] | A2.01B, A8.01 Door Schedule | The door between Garage **15** and the conditioned interior is scheduled as an ordinary solid-core interior door with no fire-rating or self-closing requirement indicated. | High  
[E-5] | A5.01, A5.10, Specs p.3 | Pool barrier documentation is incomplete because the set does not show a consistent compliant barrier strategy or any self-closing/self-latching gate requirements for the pool gates. | High  
[E-6] | A5.10/2 | The east deck elevation calls out a **26"** cable railing at the stair, which is below typical residential handrail/guard heights if that note is intentional. | High  
[E-7] | A8.01 Window Schedule, A7.02, A3.20-A3.22 | The door and window schedules do not identify any tempered/safety glazing requirements at doors, baths, low glazing, or stair-adjacent glazing. | High  
[E-8] | A8.01 Window Schedule, A2.01A-B, A2.02 | Sleeping-room windows are not identified as emergency escape openings and the schedule does not provide clear-opening or sill-height information to verify compliance. | Moderate  
[E-9] | A3.22, A5.10 | Stair drawings show railings/guards but do not provide separate handrail criteria, profiles, or extension information for stair safety review. | Moderate  

### F. Constructability

[F-1] | A5.01, A5.10, A5.20, Specs p.10 | The pool, retaining wall, deck, drainage, and mechanical-area interfaces are repeatedly left **"to be coordinated with pool,"** so the primary pool/deck edge cannot be built from the documents alone. | Certain  
[F-2] | A5.01 | The removable deck panel for pool-cover access is shown only in plan with no framing, support, hardware, or removal detail. | Certain  
[F-3] | A4.13/5, A3.20, Specs p.1 | The concealed steel-supported porch/entry overhang is a custom condition, but the architectural details do not show how the steel fits within the wall assembly or connects to surrounding finishes. | High  
[F-4] | A1.01, A2.01A-B, Specs p.3 | Wet-laid and dry-laid **SS-3** paths, patio, and ramp are called out, but no build-up, edge restraint, or drainage details are provided for these exterior paving systems. | High  
[F-5] | A3.01-A3.03, Specs p.4 | Rain chains, collection pots, and underground leaders are part of the design intent, but no details show overflow control, pot construction, cleanouts, or pipe connection at grade. | High  
[F-6] | A5.10-A5.20, Specs p.10 | The specs require the exposed above-ground pool walls to be finished, but no drawing identifies that finish or how it terminates at grade, deck, and pool edges. | High  
[F-7] | A2.01A, Specs p.6 | The master bath hydronic floor heat is labeled **"bidder design layout"** on the architectural plan without a defined manifold/control location or coordination criteria with the floor assembly. | Moderate  

### G. Completeness and clarity

[G-1] | G1.00 title sheet, Specs p.1 | The title sheet directory and the specification cover leave key project-identification fields such as owner, architect, and structural engineer blank. | Certain  
[G-2] | G0.11 | The code summary leaves the planning application number blank. | Certain  
[G-3] | Specs p.1 | The specification booklet includes placeholder text under **"Plan Changes"** and an incomplete drawing-set reference instead of finalized project information. | Certain  
[G-4] | A8.01, Specs booklet | The door schedule leaves hardware blank for most doors and the provided specs do not supply hardware sets, so door hardware scope is largely undefined. | Certain  
[G-5] | A6.01 | The lighting fixture schedule leaves all fixture manufacturers and model numbers as **TBD**. | Certain  
[G-6] | Specs pp.1-10 | Major scope-defining selections remain unresolved in the specs, including HVAC basis, HERO certification, stains/colors, carpet, hardware, countertop thickness, and pool technical criteria, which prevents a clean fixed-price bid. | High  
[G-7] | A8.01 Room Finish Schedule | The bathroom finish schedule comments end with **"FOR SHOWER FINISHES SEE"** but do not identify any destination sheet or specification reference. | Certain  
[G-8] | A8.01 Room Finish Schedule, Specs booklet | Finish key **T-1** appears in the room finish schedule, but no drawing legend or spec section defines what **T-1** is. | High  
[G-9] | G0.01, A1.01 | Notes such as **"site verify Level One height"** and **"driveway location verify in field"** defer primary new-construction design decisions that should be fixed in the contract documents. | High  
[G-10] | Specs p.6, A7.10 | The specs reference sheet **A8.10** for the reading nook bunk detail even though no **A8.10** exists in the set. | Certain  
[G-11] | Specs p.10 | The pool technical specifications are explicitly deferred for later determination with the subcontractor, leaving a major scope item incomplete at bid time. | Certain  
[G-12] | A2.01A, A2.01B, G0.01 | The notation system is inconsistent, using tags such as **W1/E1** in notes while assemblies are formally designated **W-1/E-1** elsewhere. | High  

**Summary**

Overall, this set is conceptually developed but not bid-complete, with numerous coordination gaps between plans, schedules, details, and the narrative specification booklet. The three biggest risk areas are **(1) code/life-safety documentation at the garage and pool barrier, (2) unresolved pool/deck/retaining-wall/mechanical coordination, and (3) major drawing/spec conflicts on core products and systems such as windows, doors, insulation, hardware, and low-voltage scope**. Low-slope roofing and custom envelope conditions are also under-detailed and likely to generate RFIs and water-management risk. As issued, the package would likely produce substantial bidder assumptions, change-order exposure, and field interpretation disputes.
